concept: Shy / Reserved / Hesitant in Social Situations
description: Nervous or timid in the company of others; reserved and hesitant. 'Nilua' names the quality of shyness—the holding back that comes from sensitivity, the quietness in unfamiliar company.
sound_symbolism: The word draws inward. 'Ni' begins with contained presence, self-protected. 'Lu' flows gently, holding back. Finally 'a' opens slightly, vulnerable but cautious.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'nilua kana' (shy child). Opposite of 'kaeshu' (bold). Shyness in Phi is not flaw but sensitivity—different from fearfulness.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Shy speakers need space—patient listening draws out those who hesitate to speak.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Shyness can reflect awareness—sensitivity to how one's presence affects others.
